Platform & Data.narrative: This family ensures your team can access inventory data anytime, anywhere, and keeps customer and supplier information secure and compliant with Singapore's PDPA rules. Your warehouse staff can check stock on their phones, your finance team can export data to accounting software, and you maintain a complete audit trail of who changed what. For a Singapore SME, this is non-negotiable: you need mobile access for fast-moving operations, PDPA compliance to avoid regulatory fines, and seamless data flow to your accountant.

Inventory Core.narrative: This family gives you a single source of truth for every item you stock — from SKU codes and quantities on hand to financial valuation and traceability through lot numbers. When you do a stock count or receive goods, barcode scanning speeds up the work and cuts errors. A Singapore SME needs this to avoid stockouts that lose sales, prevent shrinkage and theft, and ensure you can trace products back to suppliers if quality issues arise.

Procurement.narrative: This family automates the process of ordering from suppliers — you create a purchase order, track its status, and manage supplier relationships all in one place. You can see which suppliers deliver on time, negotiate better terms, and avoid duplicate orders. For a Singapore SME importing goods or managing multiple suppliers, this cuts procurement cycle time, reduces maverick spending, and ensures you always have the right stock at the right cost.

Warehouse & Logistics.narrative: This family orchestrates the physical movement of goods — from receiving inbound shipments and storing them in the right location, to picking and packing customer orders and handling returns. It covers multi-warehouse operations, dropshipping, and even light assembly of kits. A Singapore SME with multiple locations or complex fulfillment needs (e.g., e-commerce plus B2B) relies on this to ship faster, reduce picking errors, and manage returns without chaos.

Planning & Compliance.narrative: This family helps you forecast demand, set reorder points so you never run out, calculate the true landed cost of imports (including duties and freight), and ensure quality standards are met at every step. It also handles cross-border compliance — HS codes, export licences, and trade agreements. A Singapore SME that imports goods or sells across borders must use this to avoid customs delays, manage seasonal demand swings, and keep quality consistent.

Analytics & Intelligence.narrative: This family provides dashboards and reports that show you how fast inventory is turning, how often you fulfil orders on time, and where bottlenecks are in your supply chain. You can spot trends, compare supplier performance, and make data-driven decisions on stock levels and procurement. A Singapore SME competing in fast-moving markets (retail, F&B, e-commerce) needs visibility into these metrics to stay agile and cut working capital tied up in slow-moving stock.

AI & Machine Learning for Supply Chain.narrative: This family uses advanced algorithms to predict demand from external signals (weather, social media, events), detect anomalies in supplier performance, and optimise inventory automatically. It is a 'nice-to-have' that becomes valuable only when you have large volumes, complex multi-SKU operations, or highly volatile demand. Most Singapore SMEs can operate effectively without it in the early stages; it is a future upgrade once you have mastered the core capabilities.
